meaning: n.vi.掠过,迁徙,迅速飞过,(鸟,蝙蝠等)飞来飞去
examples: With the originals, volunteers' eyes tended to stay longer on certain places in the image, but with the altered versions they would flit across a piece more rapidly.
He's prone to flit between subjects with amazing ease.
Lightning floodlights gnarled trees and a giant iron bell. Monks with umbrellas flit about in robes and slippers.
The experience is almost dizzying, as the birds flit about like Tinker Bells of the forest.
Swimming along the edge of a reef wall, vast shoals flit at jagged angles around each other.
